title: Mobility Anisotropy Reshapes Self-Propelled Motion
abstract: We exactly solve the nonequilibrium dynamics of a harmonically trapped self-propelled particle with anisotropic translational mobility in two dimensions, relevant to rodlike microswimmers and wheeled robots. The mean displacement and MSD reveal a quasi-steady plateau with vanishing fluctuations in the high-persistence regime. An exact calculation of steady-state fourth moment yields a negative excess kurtosis that varies non-monotonically with the ratio of mechanical to rotational relaxation timescales. This gives rise to a strictly sub-Gaussian steady-state position distribution, in which the particle with anisotropic mobility, in high persistence regime, is displaced into the high-potential region lying outside the stationary contour set by the activity and harmonic confinement. This is further corroborated by the relaxation of the MSD from the quasi-steady plateau to the steady-state regime.
summary: This paper provides an exact solution for the dynamics of anisotropic self-propelled particles in a harmonic trap. It demonstrates that mobility anisotropy can drive particles into high-potential regions, resulting in a sub-Gaussian distribution. This work is significant for understanding the behavior of microswimmers and autonomous robots in confined environments.
main_problem: The study investigates how anisotropic translational mobility affects the nonequilibrium dynamics and steady-state distribution of a self-propelled particle in a harmonic trap.
main_result: The authors find that anisotropic mobility leads to a sub-Gaussian steady-state position distribution and a negative excess kurtosis, causing particles to be displaced into high-potential regions in the high-persistence regime.
method: The authors provide an exact analytical solution to the nonequilibrium dynamics of the system.
model_or_system: A two-dimensional self-propelled particle (relevant to rodlike microswimmers or wheeled robots) subject to anisotropic translational mobility and a harmonic confining potential.
key_observables: Mean displacement, Mean Squared Displacement (MSD), fourth moment, excess kurtosis, and position distribution.
important_parameters: Ratio of mechanical to rotational relaxation timescales, persistence regime, and the degree of mobility anisotropy.
main_assumptions: The system is modeled as a self-propelled particle in a 2D harmonic trap with anisotropic mobility.
paper_structure: The paper presents an exact solution for the dynamics, analyzes the MSD and mean displacement, calculates the steady-state fourth moment and kurtosis, and discusses the resulting sub-Gaussian position distribution.
